hypotheses.statement: Post-2022 SPY 0DTE ORB debit verticals beat SPY buy-and-hold risk-adjusted without conditioning on macro or regime state.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: Opening-range breakouts may reflect early order-flow imbalance after the 0DTE listing expansion.
testable_predictions: Unconditional ORB returns are positive after implementable costs.
OOS risk-adjusted return beats same-trade-calendar SPY.
The effect is not dependent on removing macro or stress regimes.
validation_criteria: E2 requires MinTRL/PSR versus SR0=0 and same-trade-calendar SPY.
E2 requires implementable PnL, big-day survival, regime coverage, and benchmark drawdown comparison.
falsification_criteria: Unconditional OOS implementable PnL is negative with no validation path versus benchmark null.
Mechanism autopsy shows unconditional pooling mixes incompatible macro/stress mechanisms.
required_data: Existing Mar-Dec 2023 in-sample and Jan-Dec 2024 OOS data are sufficient to reject this stated form.

mintrl_falsify.notes: Benchmark-null validation is undefined because observed Sharpe is below the meaningful null.

evidence.conclusion: ยังสรุปไม่ได้
ยังสรุปไม่ได้

decision_log.notes: Unconditional H-A1 is retired; H-A2 is the allowed resurrection with a new macro-conditioned prediction.

hypotheses.statement: The ORB edge, if it exists, is macro-conditioned: excluding high-importance scheduled macro days should improve implementable risk-adjusted OOS returns because non-macro breakout days reflect cleaner order-flow imbalance.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: On scheduled macro days, the opening range can be dominated by anticipatory positioning and post-release repricing rather than persistent intraday imbalance.
testable_predictions: Macro-day trades remain weak or negative in fresh data.
Retained no-macro trades remain positive after cost and big-day removal.
The effect is not concentrated in one VIX/trend bucket unless the hypothesis is explicitly scope-restricted.
validation_criteria: Use untouched validation data or DSR accounting for the inherited 9-trial M5.5 search.
Pass MinTRL/PSR against SR0=0 and same-trade-calendar SPY.
Use implementable PnL, big-day dependency, and regime coverage or explicit scope restriction.
falsification_criteria: Fresh retained no-macro trades have implementable PnL <= 0 after reaching MinTRL_falsify.
Macro-day/no-macro mechanism reverses in fresh data.
required_data: Pre-registered H-A2 re-analysis from existing M5.5 evidence.
If justified, top 2-3 high-VIX months from 2022 H2 after cost guard check.

mintrl_falsify.method: compute from retained no-macro return distribution before paid validation data

evidence.conclusion: ยังสรุปไม่ได้
ยังสรุปไม่ได้
evidence.notes: Pre-registered v2 re-analysis of existing M5.5 evidence. Inherits 9-trial search contamination and remains blocked by DSR/sample/regime gaps.

evidence.conclusion: ยังสรุปไม่ได้
evidence.notes: Aug 2024 high-VIX diagnostic found complete VIX/market-data coverage but zero ORB candidate trades during high-VIX dates; this is ORB silence, not a VIX labeling gap.

decision_log.notes: H-A2.2 completed using existing M5.5 evidence only; no new paid data. Result remains E1 and cannot support operational validation.

H-A2.3 completed on cached Aug 2024 data only. VIX labels and market data are present, but ORB produced zero candidate trades on both same-day high-VIX diagnostic dates and prior-close high-VIX ex-ante dates.

hypotheses.statement: Capped-risk put ratio spreads are feasible and profitable under $300 allocation and $20 risk budget on a $1,000 account.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: Selling short-dated downside skew may have premium edge if tail risk is capped by a protective wing.
testable_predictions: A meaningful share of trades fits the $300 allocation and $20 risk budget.
Implementable PnL is not structurally negative after costs.
validation_criteria: Defined-risk spread construction.
Small-account sizing feasibility.
Implementable PnL and tail-risk metrics.
falsification_criteria: Zero trades fit the current sizing rule.
Implementable PnL remains materially negative under the tested current-account assumptions.
required_data: Existing M4.2 Sub-System B feasibility report.

mintrl_falsify.notes: The feasibility blocker is deterministic: 0 trades fit sizing.

decision_log.notes: Current small-account H-B1 is killed; H-B2 tests the structure at larger simulated scale.

hypotheses.statement: The put-ratio-with-wing structure may have positive expectancy at simulated $10k to $25k scale where strike granularity and premium-to-account ratios stop binding.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: If short-dated downside skew is overpriced, a capped-risk structure may monetize it at sufficient account size while preserving survival through the protective wing.
testable_predictions: Cost drag is not the dominant share of gross edge at all wing widths.
Implementable PnL and ES95-adjusted return are not negative at both simulated scales.
Results are not dependent on fractional contracts or hidden undefined risk.
validation_criteria: Pre-register $10k and $25k account sizes.
Pre-register wing grid and sizing rule.
Record search log and DSR or DSR blocker.
Report implementable PnL, ES95, drawdown, cost drag, and sample labels.
falsification_criteria: Implementable PnL and ES95-adjusted return are negative at both simulated scales with adequate falsification sample.
Cost drag exceeds 60% of gross at all wing widths.
required_data: Already-cached Sub-System B data from Mar-Dec 2023 and Jan-Dec 2024.

mintrl_falsify.method: compute from the 412-trade Sub-System B return distribution after pre-registered rerun

evidence.notes: Supports H-B2 motivation but does not test H-B2 scale.

decision_log.notes: Zero-cost rerun track using cached data.

hypotheses.statement: A signed-OI gamma proxy aggregated from OPRA open interest and self-computed Greeks carries economic signal: high positive proxy days should exhibit attenuated realized intraday volatility.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: Positive aggregate dealer-style gamma exposure can damp realized volatility through hedging flows, while negative gamma can amplify moves. Current data supports only proxy validity, not true dealer inventory claims.
testable_predictions: Proxy terciles show monotonic or sign-consistent realized variance differences.
The relation survives across at least 10 dates and at least 3 regimes.
Coverage passes pre-registered v2 gates while v1 fail remains permanent.
validation_criteria: Issue GAMMA_AGGREGATION_VALIDATION_POLICY.md v2 before rerun.
Report raw-row and bucket-weighted coverage side by side.
Pass timestamp discipline, stability, economic-sign validation, and search-log gates.
falsification_criteria: No monotonic or sign-consistent relation between proxy tercile and realized 10:00-15:45 SPY variance across the pre-registered multi-regime date set.
required_data: OPRA statistics/OI days for the 12-date set.
Existing quote and SPY bar cache for those dates.

mintrl_falsify.notes: This is a proxy-validity hypothesis using multi-date regime/sign consistency before strategy-return MinTRL.

evidence.conclusion: ยังสรุปไม่ได้
evidence.notes: One-date diagnostic: timestamp/search-log pass, coverage/stability/economic-sign blocked.

decision_log.notes: Field-unlock track; no NOVI/net-gamma strategy use before H-G1 passes.

hypotheses.statement: LLM sentiment, market-impact, and volatility-relevance scores over timestamp-clean pre-entry news are stable across repeated calls/prompt families and add information beyond VIX plus macro calendar.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: Language models may compress qualitative news context into continuous sentiment/impact/volatility scores that deterministic calendars cannot capture, but only if stability and contamination controls pass first.
testable_predictions: Repeated calls have acceptable dispersion for the selected prompt family.
Masking/anonymization reduces event memorization without destroying signal.
Scores correlate with same-day realized volatility or adverse tails after controlling for VIX and macro calendar.
validation_criteria: Use real timestamp-clean news cases only.
Compare prompt-template families and masking policies.
Run 5-call stability where cost allows.
Record model id, cutoff, document timestamps, fetch window, masking policy, and contamination probe.
falsification_criteria: Scores add no information beyond VIX/macro baselines.
required_data: Deterministic VIX and macro calendar baseline features.

evidence.conclusion: ยังสรุปไม่ได้

decision_log.notes: Design work can proceed; live research is blocked until timestamp-clean news exists.

hypotheses.statement: LLM prompts fed only chronological price/quote context may improve exit timing versus fixed TP/SL rules.
hypotheses.economic_rationale: A model might summarize intraday price/quote context into a dynamic exit assessment, but this must be separated from H-L1 and tested against strict chronological controls.
testable_predictions: Parse validity stays above the pre-registered threshold.
Any improvement survives ablation against fixed TP/SL grids.
No future same-day aggregate or post-decision data enters the prompt.
validation_criteria: Separate pre-registration.
Strict timestamp controls.
Search log and cost guard.
No mixing with news/sentiment prompts.
falsification_criteria: Cost or latency makes the branch impractical.
required_data: Chronological price and quote context at decision timestamps.
A design document before live LLM calls.

decision_log.notes: Dormant until H-L1 measurement validity is designed and news cases exist.
